verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (Kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease. It is synthesized as a preproprotein, trafficked through the ER/Golgi, and secreted into the extracellular space/fluids (notably prostatic fluid and seminal plasma). It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any documented stable outer-leaflet anchoring mechanism. It may transiently associate with cell surfaces or ECM components via reversible non-covalent interactions, but this does not constitute stable outer-leaflet accessibility by its own mechanism. Its only therapeutic surface story would be secreted/soluble targeting or pMHC, not direct surface protein engagement.
